commit.message:
feat(config): move runtime state inside .orc/ by default

Worktrees and logs previously lived under ~/.cache/orc/<repo-name>/,
which caused name collisions for projects that share the same directory
name and mixed unrelated concerns in one cache tree.

New defaults (all overridable via config.yaml or env vars):
- Worktrees: .orc/worktrees/<branch>   (was ~/.cache/orc/<name>/<branch>)
- Logs:      .orc/logs/orc.log         (was ~/.cache/orc/orc.log)

Changes:
- config.py: WORKTREE_BASE defaults to agents_dir/worktrees; new LOG_DIR
  var (agents_dir/logs); DEV_WORKTREE drops the REPO_ROOT.name segment
- git.py: _feature_worktree_path drops REPO_ROOT.name segment
- logger.py: setup() gains default_log_file param so callers inject the
  config-derived path without overriding ORC_LOG_FILE/ORC_LOG_DIR env vars
- cli/__init__.py: passes _cfg.LOG_DIR / orc.log as default log path
- cli/logs.py: uses _cfg.LOG_DIR instead of _CACHE_DIR
- config.yaml template: updated orc-worktree-base comment; added orc-log-dir
- .orc/.gitignore template: adds worktrees/ and logs/
- bootstrap.py: worktrees and logs added to _UPGRADE_PRESERVE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct ECPG vs 站內付2.0 terminology across guides

ECPG is the EC Payment Gateway platform (covers 站內付2.0, 幕後授權,
幕後取號, 綁卡, etc.); 站內付2.0 is one specific product within ECPG.
The following incorrect equations have been corrected:

guides/14:
- Callout title: '正在串接站內付 2.0（ECPG）？' → explicitly list all
  ECPG services (站內付2.0、幕後授權、幕後取號)
- RqHeader rule: attributed to '站內付2.0' only → corrected to 'ECPG
  各服務' (applies platform-wide)

guides/15:
- §14 title: '站內付2.0 404 雙 Domain 錯誤' → 'ECPG 404 雙 Domain 錯誤'
  (dual-domain architecture is ECPG-wide, not 站內付2.0-specific)
- §14 body: updated to reflect all ECPG services are affected
- All anchor references to §14 updated accordingly

guides/16:
- URL table: '站內付2.0（ECPG）' / '站內付請款' → 'ECPG（Token 取得/
  建立交易）' / 'ECPG（查詢/授權/請退款）'
- Dual-domain warning: '站內付2.0 使用...' → 'ECPG 各服務使用...'
- Section heading: 'ECPG 站內付2.0 上線前專屬清單' → '站內付 2.0 上線
  前專屬清單' (checklist items are 站內付2.0-specific)
- PCI table: '站內付2.0（ECPG）' → '站內付 2.0（ECPG 服務之一）'

commit.message:
fix(dispatcher): only dispatch QA when coder signals done via close_task.sh

Previously any commit on a feature branch (including WIP commits) would
trigger QA dispatch. This caused QA agents to be spawned prematurely
when coder-N had uncommitted or work-in-progress commits.

Root cause: the fallback in _derive_task_state() returned 'qa' for all
unrecognised commit messages, and route() in the state machine mapped
CODER_WORK to 'qa' alongside CODER_DONE.

Fix:
- state_machine.py: CODER_WORK now routes to 'coder' (still working);
  only CODER_DONE routes to 'qa'
- git.py: fallback returns 'coder' with a 'not yet signalled done'
  message instead of dispatching QA
- Updated all affected tests; added QA-context test to reach 100%
  coverage of the new 'elif agent_name == "qa"' branch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: batch predict_proba to fix simulation timeout in CI

Pre-compute win probabilities for all n*(n-1)/2 matchup pairs upfront
using a single batch predict_proba call per model, then run simulations
using O(1) dict lookups.

Root cause: simulate_once_fast called predict_proba once per game
(~1260 calls/sim × 1000 sims = 1.26M calls), each triggering ~28
sklearn check_array validation passes through the isotonic calibration
chain. This made 1000 sims take ~13 min locally → >15 min on Actions.

Fix: precompute_matchup_probs() builds a 2016-row feature matrix
(64×63/2 unique pairs) and calls predict_proba once per model.
simulate_once_precomputed() does pure dict lookups per game.

Result: 1000 sims in ~1.4s locally (was ~780s estimated), well within
the 15-minute CI timeout on any runner speed.

Also adds batch_predict_prob / batch_predict_prob_routed helpers and
bumps workflow timeout-minutes 15→20 as a safety margin.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent newsletter popup for logged-in users (stale closure)

The useEffect setting up popup triggers had [] deps, capturing user
as null on mount. By the time Firebase auth resolved, the timer/scroll
handlers were already active and never re-checked the user state.

Fix: track user in a ref (userRef) and check it in all trigger
callbacks (checkAndShow, exit-intent, page-view counter). Also
auto-dismiss the popup if the user signs in while it's visible.

* fix: handle new CompiledNode variants from datalogic-rs 4.0.18

datalogic-rs 4.0.18 added CompiledVar, CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex
and CompiledStringConcat variants to CompiledNode. The walk_node_for_vars
match was non-exhaustive. Add a wildcard arm that conservatively returns
false (send full context) for any unknown variants.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(evaluator): handle CompiledVar/CompiledExists nodes and fix getrandom for WASM

datalogic-rs 4.0.18 introduced dedicated CompiledVar and CompiledExists nodes
replacing BuiltinOperator { opcode: Var/Exists }. Update walk_node_for_vars to
extract variable paths from PathSegment slices so required_context_keys works
correctly and optimization tests pass.

Also replace the wasm_js getrandom feature (browser-only) with the unsupported
cfg backend via .cargo/config.toml. This eliminates __wbg_getRandomValues imports
that broke wasmtime, Chicory, and wazero runtimes. The evaluator never calls
getrandom at runtime since murmurhash3 is fully deterministic.

Regenerate flagd_evaluator.wasm with the clean WASM binary.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(evaluator): update CompiledNode variant matching for datalogic-rs fork

Switch to tuple-variant syntax for CustomOperator, StructuredObject,
CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex, CompiledThrow to match the refactored
struct layout in the patched datalogic-rs fork.

Use [patch.crates-io] with git URL pointing to fix/wasm-wasmbind-unconditional
branch in aepfli/datalogic-rs, which removes the unconditional chrono/wasmbind
dependency on wasm32 targets. This eliminates __wbg_* browser imports that break
wasmtime, Chicory, and wazero. PR upstream: GoPlasmatic/datalogic-rs#48.

Regenerate flagd_evaluator.wasm — binary is now clean of wasm-bindgen imports.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wasm): use datalogic-rs 4.0.19, no wasm-bindgen imports

Switch from git-patched fork to registry datalogic-rs 4.0.19 which fixed
the WASM browser dependency issue properly: the 'wasm' feature (chrono/wasmbind)
is now opt-in and disabled by default.

Use getrandom_backend="unsupported" cfg flag since nothing in the evaluation
path calls getrandom at runtime (ahash uses compile-time-rng, murmurhash3 is
deterministic). This produces a fully clean WASM binary with zero __wbg_*
imports.

Update walk_node_for_vars to use 4.0.19's tuple-variant CompiledNode API
(CustomOperator, StructuredObject, CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex,
CompiledThrow).

Result: flagd_evaluator.wasm has no wasm-bindgen imports at all — compatible
with Chicory, wazero, Wasmtime, and any other non-browser WASM runtime.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wasm): provide custom getrandom backend to prevent runtime panic

ahash's default features include runtime-rng, which calls getrandom at
startup to XOR a runtime-random value into the compile-time seed. With
getrandom_backend="unsupported", this panicked (unreachable trap) on
every update_state call across all language runtimes.

Switch to getrandom_backend="custom" and define __getrandom_v03_custom
to fill with zeros. This is safe: ahash already has a strong compile-time
seed via const-random, and the evaluator has no cryptographic randomness
requirements. Result: zero __wbg_* imports, no runtime panics.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor and enhance Commissioning APIs (#3330)

* Enhance multi discovered device commissioning

We now try devices one after the other always one address and so give all devices a better chance to be tried within the timeout. We also ensure to continue (but remove the device) on PASE errors (e,g wrong passcode).

Also some code was streamlined between the legacy and node driven path. Also node driven commissionings can now handle multiple targets by chosing the relevant node depending on the commissioning result.

This change can also be a basis to easiely implement Multi-QR code commissioning in a later step

* formatting

* Adjust logic according to initial feedback

* Enhance multi-discovered device commissioning with parallel PASE and abort

- Rewrite CommissioningConnection as factory function with static and dynamic modes:
  - Static mode (pre-known addresses): launch all candidates immediately in parallel
  - Dynamic mode: poll discovered devices every 200ms, track permanently-failed addresses
  - Per-device AbortController map: UnexpectedDataError cancels sibling address attempts
  - Race loser cleanup: call initiateForceClose on losing PASE sessions to avoid 60s lockout
  - Discovery poll errors logged with context instead of silently swallowed
  - lastNonRetryableError tracks credential failures for clean UnexpectedDataError propagation

- Thread AbortSignal through the PASE stack:
  - PaseMessenger: abort-aware send/receive using Abort.race
  - PaseClient: abort.attempt() around each async step, sends InvalidParam on abort
  - ControllerCommissioner: abort? and continueCommissioningAfterPase? options
  - sendError intentionally not abort-bounded to notify device before closing

- Add CommissioningDiscovery parallel commissioning:
  - Runs CommissioningClient.commission() per candidate in parallel
  - #paseWon flag gates the continueCommissioningAfterPase callback
  - Winner triggers abort signal to cancel remaining candidates
  - try/finally cleanup ensures pending promises resolve on error

- Add WiFi/Thread/regulatory credentials to CommissioningClient.BaseCommissioningOptions:
  - wifiNetwork?, threadNetwork?, regulatoryLocation?, regulatoryCountryCode?
  - Wired through CommissioningClient.commission() into LocatedNodeCommissioningOptions

- Add Peers.forDescriptor(descriptor: RemoteDescriptor): Promise<ClientNode>:
  - Uses ClientNodeFactory.find() to find an existing matching node
  - If found, refreshes addresses and discovery data via setStateOf
  - If not found, creates a new ClientNode via factory.create()
  - Uses RemoteDescriptor (new-style interface) instead of legacy CommissionableDevice
  - Follows the existing forAddress() naming pattern

- Migrate CommissioningController.commissionNode() to use new path for all routes:
  - Pre-discovered/known-address path: peers.forDescriptor() + clientNode.commission()
    replacing direct ControllerCommissioner.commission() + peers.forAddress()
  - Discovery data (SII/SAI/SAT) automatically propagated via CommissioningClient.descriptor
  - commissionedAt set by CommissioningClient.commission() (removed redundant setStateOf)
  - OTA provider location handled automatically by CommissioningClient
  - Pure-discovery path unchanged: CommissioningDiscovery via peers.commission()
  - Removes ControllerCommissioner, Time imports from CommissioningController
  - Fixes identifierData type narrowing in pure-discovery path

- Restore Outdoor regulatory default accidentally changed to Indoor

- Add 10 CommissioningConnection tests covering all major code paths

* formatting

* fix(ble): two bugs in NobleBleChannel connect/disconnect path

**Missing return after successful channel creation**
After resolveOnce() succeeds in connectHandler the service loop continued
to completion, fell through to the rejectOnce() fallback intended for the
"no Matter characteristics found" case, and logged:
  "Already resolved or rejected, ignore error: Peripheral … does not have
   the required Matter characteristics"
Add return; in the success path so the fallback is only reached when the
loop genuinely finds no suitable service.

**Swapped error labels + unsubscribe race on BTP session close**
The error callbacks for unsubscribeAsync() and disconnectAsync() had their
log labels swapped.  Additionally, when the remote side closes the BTP
session first Noble fires the disconnect event and its _withDisconnectHandler
rejects any pending BLE operation (including unsubscribeAsync) with
"Disconnected unknown".  The previous error handler just logged and stopped,
never calling disconnectAsync().
Fix: use .catch() so an unsubscribe failure is logged but the code still
proceeds to disconnectAsync() if the peripheral is still connected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: empty BLE deviceIdentifier causing re-commissioning of existing node

On macOS CoreBluetooth hides real BLE peripheral addresses, so the BLE
scanner emits deviceIdentifier: "" for every discovered device.

RemoteDescriptor.is() treated "" as a valid identifier ("" !== undefined),
so factory.find() could match it against a previously commissioned node
that was also discovered via BLE (also stored deviceIdentifier: "").
CommissioningDiscovery.onDiscovered() would then receive the old
commissioned node and CommissioningClient.commission() would throw
"is already commissioned".

Fixes:
- RemoteDescriptor.is(): treat empty-string deviceIdentifier the same as
  absent — an empty string means "identity unknown" and must not be used
  to match unrelated nodes.
- Discovery.ts: second safety net — if factory.find() returns a node that
  is already commissioned (node.lifecycle.isCommissioned), treat it as no
  match and let the flow create a fresh node.
- CommissioningClient.ts: filter BLE addresses out of the "Operational
  address changed" log line; ble:// is meaningless on macOS and clutters
  logs with empty-string addresses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(polyfill): SuppressedError installed as arrow function, breaking new E()

TypeScript's __callDispose polyfill (generated for await using) calls
new E(...) where E = globalThis.SuppressedError.  The suppressed-error
polyfill was installing an arrow function (factory) as the global, but
arrow functions cannot be used as constructors.  This caused:
  "E is not a constructor"
whenever two errors occurred simultaneously during an await using scope
(e.g. commissioning reconnect failure + failsafe reset failure), crashing
the cleanup path instead of wrapping both errors in a SuppressedError.

Fix: install the class directly.  The factory pattern added no value —
TypeScript's generated code always uses new, and instanceof checks work
correctly against the class itself.

commit.message:
test: add end-to-end tests for full publish-receive pipeline

Add 28 E2E tests exercising the complete NimBus message flow using
an in-memory loopback transport (InMemoryBus) that simulates Azure
Service Bus without requiring real infrastructure.

Test categories:
- BasicPublishReceiveTests (7): single event publish/receive, property
  round-trip, message completion, deterministic message IDs
- EventRoutingTests (4): multiple event types routed to correct handlers,
  unknown event type handling
- ErrorHandlingTests (5): exception handling, transient exceptions,
  session blocking, retry policies
- PipelineAndLifecycleTests (6): pipeline behavior wrapping, lifecycle
  observer notifications (OnReceived/OnCompleted/OnFailed)
- BatchAndValidationTests (6): batch publishing, event validation

Infrastructure:
- InMemoryBus: ISender loopback with SQL Rule Action simulation
- FakeServiceBusSession: IServiceBusSession test double
- EndToEndFixture: wires PublisherClient -> InMemoryBus -> MessageHandler
- TestLoggerProvider: captures log output for assertions
